The '''Foerost Shipyards''' were vast and ancient [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]]. They were originally used by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], but were often targeted or taken over by the Republic's enemies.

==Description==
The Foerost Shipyards were located in high orbit above the uninhabited planet Foerost. Materials were collected from the planet below and shipped into orbit where they were assembled into [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. The shipyards of Foerost were among the most successful large-scale construction facilities in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TSW" />

==History==
The Foerost Shipyards were among the oldest shipyards of the galaxy.<ref name="TSW" /> Foerost and its shipyards served as headquarters for the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Tionese War]], circa [[24,000 BBY]].<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

The Foerost Shipyards were engulfed by [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] fleet during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>. During the [[Old Sith Wars]], [[Zentine Dynamics]] produced [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|''Ministry''-class orbital shuttles]] in the Foerost Shipyards.<ref name="KotORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> In [[3996 BBY]], during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] led [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] and [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] forces in a [[Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)|raid]] on the shipyards. They ravaged the shipyards and stole 300 of the newest Republic warships with which to [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|attack]] the Republic capital [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TSW" /> The Foerost Shipyards were later repaired and returned into Republic control. Decades later, in [[3959 BBY]], another Sith Lord, [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]], [[Battle of Foerost (Jedi Civil War)|attacked]] the shipyards and seized the bulk of the Republic fleet for his own [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|armada]], initiating the [[Jedi Civil War]].<ref name="TORE" />

Millennia later, after the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Armed Forces]] were disbanded, the shipyards were sold by the Republic to the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]]. Corporate signatories of the Techno Union then used the facility for construction and testing of new materials and propulsion systems.<ref name="HNN53" /> During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the Techno Union secretly joined the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] with the objective to overthrow the Republic.<ref name="TNEC" /> In the meantime, the Techno Union tightened the security of the shipyards with thousands of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]].<ref name="HNN53" /> When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began, the Techno Union provided the Foerost Shipyards to the Separatist war effort. In response, the Republic [[Foerost Siege|blockaded]] Foerost, expecting the shipyards to decline without access to Separatist supply line. Contrariwise, Separatist engineers were able to develop and secretly build a fleet of [[Bulwark Mark I|''Bulwark''-class battleships]]. After nearly two years of blockade, the [[Bulwark Fleet]] [[Battle of Foerost (Clone Wars)|broke]] the siege of Foerost and attacked [[Imperial Center Oversector|Sector Zero]].<ref name="TNEC" /> After the defeat of the Bulwark Fleet at the [[Battle of Anaxes (21 BBY)|Battle of Anaxes]], the Republic eventually recaptured Foerost.<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|284|Foerost}}</ref>

The Foerost Shipyards still operated during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]].<ref name="TEGtW" />
